title: Equivalent fractions (fraction models)
description: Practice finding equivalent fractions. These problems show you pictures of fractions to help you out.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each rectangle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac34=\dfrac{y}{8}$

${y}=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]


![rectangle with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded. Same-sized rectangle with 8 equal parts](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/86659d3cc9161f4c1426e21dff8e5a9577c48e2d)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\maroonD{\text{eighths}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\blueD{\dfrac34}$?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![rectangle with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c66ae0afc1eeaa56dcb2e7683f39f92660187d7a)

$\blueD{\dfrac34}=\maroonD{\dfrac68}$
${y}=\maroonD6$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each rectangle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac23=\dfrac{x}9$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![rectangle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded. Same-sized rectangle with 9 equal parts](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d03084a7b87d64ea34a757f11481c9ee0e5ffa20)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\maroonD{\text{ninths}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\blueD{\dfrac23}$?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![rectangle with 2of 3 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 6 of 9 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c65f040718fd8b494110baf431113a20c0e96c20)

$\blueD{\dfrac23}=\maroonD{\dfrac69}$
$x=\maroonD6$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each rectangle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*


$\dfrac35=\dfrac{w}{10}$

$w=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![rectangle with 3 of 5 equal parts shaded. Same-sized rectangle with 10 equal parts](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/214b5cc3a6656deba0cef34bb8fab3473c9dcb5b)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\maroonD{\text{tenths}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\blueD{\dfrac35}$?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![rectangle with 3 of 5 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 6 of 10 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9c977f0f249c5e5252be6f3d312963f13940d235)

$\blueD{\dfrac35}=\maroonD{\dfrac6{10}}$
$w=\maroonD6$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each rectangle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac{t}3=\dfrac26$

$t=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![rectangle with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded. Same-sized rectangle with 3 equal parts](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b6fd61046f41dded0d57cca7267484099204beb6)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\blueD{\text{thirds}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\maroonD{\dfrac26}$?
Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![rectangle with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/387b750c352f20c0aa1e1386a9e663ee7c5ab035)

$\blueD{\dfrac13} =\maroonD{\dfrac26} $
$t=\blueD1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each rectangle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac{z}6=\dfrac12$

$z=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]


![rectangle with 1 of 2 equal parts shaded. Same-sized rectangle with 6 equal parts](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/27debaf90bf440ba3e6abcb864558061d995c5b6)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\blueD{\text{sixths}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\maroonD{\dfrac12}$?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![rectangle with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/79a9b4ea2414b73bf3d6bd9d123a117cc7acd6aa)

$\blueD{\dfrac36} =\maroonD{\dfrac12}$
$z=\blueD3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each rectangle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac{c}8=\dfrac9{12}$

$c=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![rectangle with 9 of 12 equal parts shaded. Same-sized rectangle with 8 equal parts](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec3efb6ce33b5ee5fd632207e8edb9241086dd13)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\blueD{\text{eighths}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\maroonD{\dfrac9{12}}?$

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![rectangle with 9 of 12 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b743dba3f5e3c569a3ef928a9f20f93a9f144a77)

$\blueD{\dfrac68} =\maroonD{\dfrac9{12}}$
$c=\blueD6$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each circle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac46=\dfrac{k}3$

${k}=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![circle with 4 of 6 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 3 equal parts.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c548a0fb1ab2165932bd4685822956e97ad17bc1)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\maroonD{\text{thirds}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\blueD{\dfrac46}$ ?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![circle with 4 of 6 equal parts shaded.same-sized circle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first circle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/53d23a95afa06fc5dad7b5629c3e5551a40835dc)

$\blueD{\dfrac{4}{6}}=\maroonD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$
${k}=\maroonD2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each circle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac48=\dfrac{r}2$

$r=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![circle with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 2 equal parts.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4e0199de6dd3e62370da9e620d692a8407ef1f7f)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\maroonD{\text{halves}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\blueD{\dfrac48}$ ?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![circle with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded.same-sized circle with 1 of 2 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c75c5a6a7f33cbd6a5504273a083d5ba6bb09467)

$\blueD{\dfrac{4}{8}}=\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$
$r=\maroonD1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each circle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac26=\dfrac{u}3$

$u=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![circle with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 3 equal parts.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/405bc8bbd550b936db8ad1f684e3508d65ea7886)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\maroonD{\text{thirds}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\blueD{\dfrac26}$ ?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![circle with 2 of 6 equal parts shaded.same-sized circle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first circle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9678e2d602ace028948c8524b3760358270f12e6)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{6}}=\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$
$u=\maroonD1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each circle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac{d}8 = \dfrac14$

$d=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![circle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 8 equal parts.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/08a35a3b3dafcdc3962465d3bdbabff224b0578d)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\blueD{\text{eighths}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\maroonD{\dfrac14}$?

![circle with 2 of 8 equal parts shaded.same-sized circle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first circle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb6be570bb560d99bd25711a7d78256b8301b179)

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{8}}=\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$
$d=\blueD2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each circle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac{m}{4} = \dfrac68$

$m=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![circle with 4 equal parts. same-sized circle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9140226c1e300f652ca76a9747c088e2701c4417)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\blueD{\text{fourths}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\maroonD{\dfrac68}$?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![circle with 2 of 5 equal parts shaded.same-sized circle with 4 of 10 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first circle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8e034ee28b5fdba1cd77d152ea8f9f9bc308dc4b)

$\blueD{\dfrac{3}{4}}=\maroonD{\dfrac{6}{8}}$
$m=\blueD3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Think about how we can fill in each circle below to help us find an equivalent fraction.*

$\dfrac{p}{6} = \dfrac4{8}$

$p=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![circle with 6 of 12 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 4 equal parts.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b03b9960141fe5aa489404837c69c2676dd199fd)
hints:
Equivalent fractions have the same area shaded.  

How many $\blueD{\text{sixths}}$ should we shade to cover the same area as $\maroonD{\dfrac4{8}}$?

Let's slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![circle with 6 of 12 equal parts shaded.same-sized circle with 2 of 4 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first circle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/52e5aa0d6a797eabb84862c266a64fa59c0d3a22)

$\blueD{\dfrac{3}{6}}=\maroonD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$
$p=\blueD3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which picture has an area equivalent to $\dfrac{3}{4}$ shaded?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's compare each picture to $\dfrac{3}{4}$. We can slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![rectangle with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, covering less area than the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4268939de6af25d6472125feadd7136be8675f17)

$\dfrac{3}{4} \neq \dfrac{3}{6}$

![rectangle with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, covering less area than the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c09ddf73db36ccae37773f932f93861590557452)

$\dfrac{3}{4} \neq \dfrac{1}{4}$

![rectangle with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded. Same-sized rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/60e39bbc82267285d79eeb4ae8fa85738a5ca716) 

$\dfrac{3}{4} = \dfrac{6}{8}$

This picture is equivalent to $\dfrac{3}{4}$:

![rectangle with 6 of 8 equal parts shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eecc062e6a56449623f10d94c747a1de8fe35fee)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which picture has an area equivalent to $\dfrac{8}{12}$ shaded?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's compare each picture to $\dfrac{8}{12}$. We can slide the shaded parts together to make them easier to compare.

![rectangle with 8 of 12 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 3 of 4 equal parts shaded, covering more area than the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2ae0b6bf85c321b73184000a0cb48063efc9790)

$\dfrac{8}{12} \neq \dfrac{3}{4}$

![rectangle with 8 of 12 equal parts shaded. same-sized rectangle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, covering less area than the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d1912497cc6460a40abe148cfd1482fff77fccad)

$\dfrac{8}{12} \neq \dfrac{3}{6}$

![rectangle with 8 of 12 equal parts shaded. Same-sized rectangle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/034684b062d619d36329759008c3b857b8a632af) 

$\dfrac{8}{12} = \dfrac{2}{3}$

This picture is equivalent to $\dfrac{8}{12}$:

![rectangle with 2 of 3 parts shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ba7dd1803b9b4e136f9ebbcd2c511dbdb5ef7772)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which picture has an area equivalent to $\dfrac{4}{8}$ shaded?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's compare each picture to $\dfrac{4}{8}$.

![circle with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 4 of 6 equal parts shaded, covering more area than the first circle](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ef1afebca6eafe0b033efecfcae5fb18165bcff3)

$\dfrac48 \neq \dfrac{4}{6}$

![circle with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 3 of 8 equal parts shaded, covering less area than the first circle](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1abb3a15f0cdc9d14c22c2c4db091c0223bfd95c)

$\dfrac48 \neq \dfrac{3}{8}$

![circle with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first circle](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/761130e7b73bf949bc6b40e0d9a940a5a3b33855)

$\dfrac48 = \dfrac{3}{6}$

This picture is equivalent to $\dfrac{4}{8}$:

![circle with 3 of 6 parts shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7a735b9fed1a61feee91ff1761fe3fa868433e8f)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which picture has an area equivalent to $\dfrac{1}{3}$ shaded?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's compare each picture to $\dfrac{1}{3}$.

![circle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 2 of 3 equal parts shaded, covering more area than the first circle](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0fa4c9e6e0fc0391c15ff30ae62d979111a30d35)

$\dfrac13 \neq \dfrac{2}{3}$

![circle with 1 of 3 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 1 of 4 equal parts shaded, covering less area than the first circle](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c86d642cbc8af9d7c58179831777c23c6467a7bc)

$\dfrac13 \neq \dfrac{1}{4}$

![circle with 4 of 8 equal parts shaded. same-sized circle with 3 of 6 equal parts shaded, covering the same area as the first circle](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7b002d416d31816f6b83a3a9f4a7e2fdeecb76b9)

$\dfrac13 = \dfrac{2}{6}$

This picture is equivalent to $\dfrac13$:

![circle with 2 of 6 parts shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f1c34dd0fccf7d47b2a8b1e428a0a8c03b197b5)
